title: Spinach & Black Bean Enchiladas with Homemade Red Enchilada Sauce

Enchiladas: 400 g tin black beans, drained (240 g / ~1.25 cups when drained)
Few sprigs fresh coriander (cilantro), roughly chopped
100 g cheddar cheese, grated (~1 cup when grated)

steps: Make the enchilada sauce: Place the oil in a small saucepan over a medium-low heat. Add the flour and spices, and mix thoroughly to produce a fragrant paste. Cook for a minute, stirring constantly, then add the tomato puree and cook for a further minute.

Add the vegetable stock a little at a time (about one-third at a time), mixing well until smooth before each addition. Once all the stock is incorporated, you should have a smooth, silky sauce. Season generously with black pepper. Set aside.
Cook the spinach in a large bowl in the microwave for around 1 minute, until wilted. Set aside to cool for a minute or two.

Add the black beans, spring onions, cilantro, and half of the grated cheese to a mixing bowl. When the spinach is cool enough to handle, give it a quick chop, add it to the mixture (discarding any excess liquid), and mix well to combine. Stir in about half of the enchilada sauce.
Heat the oven to 190C (Gas Mark 5 / 375F). Spread a tablespoon or two of the remaining enchilada sauce over the bottom of a baking dish (approximately 10 x 6 inches) to prevent sticking.
Lay a tortilla on a board, spoon a quarter of the filling in a line across the centre, roll it up, and place it seam-side down in the dish. Repeat with the remaining tortillas and filling.

Spread the rest of the sauce over the top of the rolled tortillas and sprinkle over the remaining grated cheese. Bake for 30-40 minutes, until the sauce is bubbling and the cheese is crisped to your liking.
